name: Strict timeouts
description: Bound how long calls can consume resources
when_to_use: Every external database HTTP queue and service call
trade_offs: Too aggressive timeouts can increase false failures
implementation_notes: Set connect read and total deadlines and propagate cancellation

name: Exponential backoff with jitter
description: Retry transient failures without synchronized retry storms
when_to_use: Network calls queues rate limits and transient 5xx failures
trade_offs: Unsafe for non-idempotent operations without keys
implementation_notes: Retry only retryable errors with max attempts jitter and idempotency

name: Circuit Breaker
description: Stop calling unhealthy dependencies temporarily
when_to_use: Unstable third-party APIs payment providers and downstream services
trade_offs: May reject requests even as dependency recovers
implementation_notes: Track rolling failures open half-open and fallback behavior

name: Bulkhead isolation
description: Limit failures in one dependency or workload from exhausting all resources
when_to_use: Multiple downstreams worker pools and tenant workloads
implementation_notes: Use separate pools queues concurrency limits and budgets

name: Saga orchestration
description: Coordinate multi-step distributed business transactions
when_to_use: Orders payments fulfillment onboarding and cross-service writes
implementation_notes: Define forward steps compensation idempotency and audit trail

name: Graceful degradation
description: Return reduced functionality when dependencies fail
when_to_use: Non-critical recommendations analytics search or personalization
trade_offs: Can hide chronic dependency failure if not monitored
implementation_notes: Make fallback explicit and alert on degraded mode

name: Backpressure and load shedding
description: Reject or slow requests before the system collapses
when_to_use: Traffic spikes worker queues streaming and high concurrency APIs
trade_offs: Some users receive 429 or degraded service
implementation_notes: Set queue limits concurrency caps and overload responses
keywords: backpressure load shedding overload queue limit

name: Dependency budget
description: Allocate latency and error budgets per downstream call
when_to_use: Composite APIs with several databases and external services
implementation_notes: Track per-dependency timeout retries and p95 latency

name: DLQ replay workflow
description: Recover failed asynchronous messages deliberately
when_to_use: Queues webhooks imports and event consumers
trade_offs: Replay tooling can re-trigger side effects
implementation_notes: Make replay idempotent auditable and permission-gated

name: Feature flag kill switch
description: Disable risky behavior without redeploy
when_to_use: New integrations expensive jobs and optional features
implementation_notes: Define owner expiry and safe fallback for every kill switch

name: Reconciliation job
description: Periodically compare and repair derived or external state
when_to_use: Payments inventory integrations and eventual consistency
trade_offs: Can mask underlying bugs if not tracked
implementation_notes: Emit discrepancy metrics and keep repair actions audited

name: Per-tenant quotas
description: Isolate noisy tenants from shared resources
when_to_use: Multi-tenant SaaS APIs workers and storage
trade_offs: Quota design can block legitimate bursts
implementation_notes: Use rate storage and concurrency quotas with overrides

name: Concurrency limiter
description: Bound concurrent work per route dependency or tenant
when_to_use: Expensive endpoints slow downstreams and worker pools
implementation_notes: Use semaphore style limiter with timeout and 429 or queue response

name: Queue max age alert
description: Alert when work waits too long
when_to_use: Background jobs webhooks and event consumers
implementation_notes: Monitor oldest message age not only queue depth

name: Hedged requests
description: Send duplicate request after delay to reduce tail latency
when_to_use: Idempotent reads to replicated services
trade_offs: Increases load and can worsen incidents
implementation_notes: Use only for safe reads with budget and cancellation

name: Adaptive timeout
description: Adjust timeout from observed latency and dependency SLO
when_to_use: Variable latency dependencies and internal services
trade_offs: Can mask degradation if too loose
implementation_notes: Set bounds and review timeout by endpoint criticality

name: Retry budget
description: Limit total retry traffic during incidents
when_to_use: High traffic services with retrying clients
implementation_notes: Cap retries as percentage of original requests

name: Poison message detection
description: Stop retrying messages that always fail
when_to_use: Queue consumers and data import pipelines
implementation_notes: Move deterministic failures to DLQ with reason

name: Separate worker pools
description: Run critical and non-critical jobs on different pools
when_to_use: Notifications billing exports reports and imports
implementation_notes: Assign queues and concurrency by priority and dependency

name: Tenant workload isolation
description: Separate heavy tenant work from shared workers
when_to_use: Enterprise SaaS noisy neighbor protection
implementation_notes: Use per-tenant queues or weighted fair scheduling

name: Compensating action
description: Undo or offset completed step after later failure
when_to_use: Saga workflows payments fulfillment and provisioning
trade_offs: Compensation may be partial or manual
implementation_notes: Define compensation for every non-transactional step

name: Manual intervention state
description: Represent workflows that need operator decision
when_to_use: Payments fraud provisioning and data repair
implementation_notes: Persist blocked state reason and safe resume action

name: Replay-safe events
description: Make event replay not corrupt state
when_to_use: Event sourcing projections and backfills
implementation_notes: Use event IDs versions and deterministic handlers

name: Static fallback data
description: Serve last known safe data when dependency fails
when_to_use: Catalog config feature flags and public content
implementation_notes: Expose freshness and alert when fallback is active

name: Canary backend release
description: Expose new backend version to small traffic slice
when_to_use: High-risk changes migrations and performance-sensitive services
trade_offs: Needs routing and metrics by version
implementation_notes: Promote only after error and latency checks pass
